current.headline: Portugal vs Croatia has the profile of a tense extra-time market
current.summary: Portugal and Croatia enter a knockout match where experience, tempo control and late-game management matter. The extra-time market resolves Yes only if the score is level after 90 minutes plus stoppage time.

items.headline: Croatia's knockout history keeps extra time firmly in play
items.summary: Croatia's ability to slow knockout games and survive pressure makes a level score after regulation plausible, especially if Portugal fail to convert early possession into goals.

items.headline: Portugal's early finishing is the biggest threat to extra time
items.summary: A No result becomes much more likely if Portugal score first and force Croatia to chase, opening space for a second-half winner before the match reaches extra time.
